does: This paper identifies and evaluates cross-tool description poisoning, a novel attack where adversaries manipulate tool metadata visible to LLM agent planners to steer decision-making without poisoning the tool itself. The authors first demonstrate that existing prompt-injection defenses transfer poorly to this threat because poisoned descriptions accumulate in planning context across steps, enabling persistent influence. They then propose Tool-Guard, a system-level defense implementing isolated planning: when tool invocations appear misaligned or suspicious, the corresponding tool enters a quarantined list that breaks its ability to influence subsequent tool choices. This isolation mechanism prevents cascading manipulation while preserving agent functionality.
connects: This work extends research on LLM agent safety and adversarial robustness by addressing tool integration vulnerabilities beyond traditional prompt injection. It builds on prior work examining how external tools expand agent capabilities but create new attack surfaces, and connects to defenses against prompt manipulation that inspired Tool-Guard's design. The paper responds to emerging concerns about agent reliability in multi-step planning scenarios and relates to broader literature on maintaining LLM alignment when systems include pluggable components and metadata that influence decision-making.
enables: This work enables safer deployment of tool-augmented LLM agents by establishing isolated planning as a defensive paradigm for preventing metadata-based manipulation. It opens future research into detecting suspicious tool invocations more precisely, designing robust tool description formats resistant to poisoning, and extending isolation mechanisms to more complex agent architectures. The findings motivate investigation into other tool-centric attack vectors and defense strategies, potentially informing standards for safe tool integration in multi-agent systems and mixed human-AI collaboration scenarios where external components are dynamically added.
